title: Routing, Selection, Translation, and Redundancy · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Connect branch, headquarters, cloud, and internet routes while diagnosing asymmetry and failover. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a routed hybrid branch design after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.
Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: network routing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Subinterfaces trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.

content.text: For Net Subinterfaces, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Subinterfaces precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network routing. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One physical interface can carry only one IP network and one VLAN.

stimulus.title: Net Subinterfaces case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

Artifact under review: a complete first version of a routed hybrid branch design while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Net Subinterfaces, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Subinterfaces applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1Q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.
